verdict: An exceptionally rhythmic and expressive chart with great flow, held back by several severe 16th-note footlocks during hold steps.

issues.problem: Holding D requires the player to use a single free foot to hit L and then R just a 16th note later (110ms), creating an extremely awkward footlock.
Holding U forces the free foot to perform a 16th-note transition from D to R, which is physically too fast for a single foot.
Holding L forces the free foot to execute a rapid 16th-note transition from U to D.
Holding D forces the free foot to execute a rapid 16th-note transition from L to R.
